reasoning: The reported vulnerability in keymap-check.sh is a false positive; the script isn't executed during makepkg build/install. Other files are static configurations or build scripts with no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged keymap-check.sh as unsafe due to a /tmp symlink vulnerability, but it's not executed in build/install context.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped binary tarball (hal-info-20091130.tar.gz) without content inspection
false_positives: qwen's unsafe verdict on keymap-check.sh: Script isn't executed during makepkg (development tool only)
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Focus on build/install hooks over development tools
Enhance model training: Clarify that non-executed scripts don't affect package safety
Fix false positive triggers: Tempfile usage in non-build scripts shouldn't fail audits
Improve binary handling: Consider lightweight extraction for source tarball inspection
